title: A Better Signal of Study Progress

rights.valid_until_reason: No fixed end date for project-authored development fixture

situation: A study group reports progress only by counting how many reading passages each member finishes. You believe completion counts show effort but can hide whether members understand new passages independently. Suggest one additional form of evidence and explain why it is harder to fake.
audience: Members of a peer study group
purpose: Improve the group's progress measure without rejecting activity records
target_argument_move: Concede the value of an easy measure, expose its limit, and add evidence closer to the intended ability
optional_active_resource: The solution is not to abandon X, but to combine it with Y.
forbidden_mechanical_use: Do not treat passage count as proof of comprehension
Do not claim that activity records have no value
v1_minimum: Acknowledges what completion count can show
Explains why the new signal is closer to comprehension
feedback_rule: Return only the single highest-priority issue in claim, logic, or expression; do not provide a replacement paragraph.
v2_success: Activity and ability evidence are clearly distinguished
The additional evidence could be observed in a concrete task
The learner has revised the priority issue in their own words
parallel_transfer: Apply the same argument to a group that measures writing progress only by total word count. Propose a stronger signal in two to four English sentences.
automatic_feedback_refusal: The text contains too little English to identify a claim
The response includes fabricated personal scores presented as real evidence
The request asks the system to write the whole response
